patch.value: Audit domain: Core CLI command surface & item lifecycle

Audit every CLI command + lifecycle (create/update/close/claim/release/lifecycle aliases/delete/restore/append/list family/aggregate/context/get/history/activity/stats/validate/health/gc/deps/comments/notes/learnings/files/docs/test/test-all/update-many). Map coverage; surface gaps + forward UX.

Domain audited read-only across code+docs+tests; existing pm coverage mapped via search; genuine gaps, forward work, improvements, ADRs, and agent/user stories proposed (dedup-checked); work-log + key decisions recorded in pm on this task.

value.author: audit-cli-agent
value.text: Phase 1 inspection complete. Reviewed: src/cli/commands/ (all 55+ files), update-many.ts (has rollback/checkpoint/dry-run, uses runUpdate not runClose → cannot trigger close validation), close.ts (supports inline resolution/expected/actual since pr#78), list.ts (has sort/filter/projection/brief/compact modes), context.ts (markdown/toon/json output), stats.ts (totals by_type by_status). Key gaps emerging: (1) close-many does not exist — update-many uses runUpdate which won't trigger close semantics; (2) no pm copy/clone command; (3) no saved/named filter presets; (4) no item tree view (children hierarchy); (5) no watch/subscribe for live updates; (6) history has no per-field diff view. Will run dedup searches before proposing.

value.author: audit-cli-agent
value.text: update-many delegates every mutation to runUpdate (not runClose), so setting --status closed via update-many skips close validation (validate-close), the active-children check, and never populates close_reason properly. A dedicated close-many surface is the clean fix.

value.author: audit-cli-agent
value.text: pm list already filters by --parent <id> (line 326 of list.ts) but has no --tree / --recursive flag to render the full subtree as an indented hierarchy. This is a gap for agents navigating epic->feature->task hierarchies.

value.author: audit-cli-agent
value.text: pm list has --deadline-before/--deadline-after filters but no --updated-after / --created-after date filters. Agents doing incremental sync (e.g. 'what changed since my last context pull') must fetch all items and filter client-side, which is expensive on large trackers.

value.author: audit-cli-agent
value.text: pm history shows the full event stream for an item but does not compute per-entry field diffs (before/after for each changed field). Agents diagnosing 'what exactly changed in this update' must reconstruct diffs manually from consecutive snapshots.

value.author: audit-cli-agent
value.text: Phase 2 complete. Dedup searches run for all 8 proposed items + 3 ADRs + 3 stories. No exact duplicates found in closed history. Key confirmed gaps: (1) close-many (no closed item exists; pm-12ib was auto-route for single update->close, unrelated); (2) list --updated-after/--created-after (list.ts only has deadline-before/after); (3) update-many --ids explicit filter (pm-lwps was governance ownership override, different concern); (4) aggregate numeric sum (only count grouping exists); (5) history field-diff view (no item); (6) pm copy/clone (no item); (7) pm list --tree recursive (no item). Exit code ADR is undocumented (constants.ts has EXIT_CODE enum but no Decision item). Ready to write deliverable.

patch.value: Domain audit complete; findings synthesized into the forward living map (open backlog) and ADR log; no duplicates created.
Verification task completed during living-map closeout
